cutover.note: The Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita 2023 (Act 45 of 2023), together with BNSS 2023 (replacing CrPC) and BSA 2023 (replacing Evidence Act), was brought into force on 1 July 2024 by MHA notifications issued in February 2024 (verified via India Code and PRS). Rule for FIRs: the substantive law follows the DATE OF OFFENCE, not the FIR date. Offences committed on/after 1 July 2024 are registered under BNS sections; offences committed before 1 July 2024 continue to be booked under IPC even if the FIR is registered later (BNS s.358 repeal-and-savings read with s.6 General Clauses Act 1897). Procedure (FIR format, investigation, e-FIR/Zero FIR under BNSS s.173) applies to all FIRs registered on/after 1 July 2024. Exception: BNS s.106(2) (hit-and-run escalated punishment) has NOT been brought into force, per the official comparative table; hit-and-run cases are booked under BNS 106(1) with MV Act sections.

offences.name: Murder — intentionally causing death; punishable with death or life imprisonment
Murder by group on ground of race/caste/community (mob lynching) — new aggravated form
Attempt to murder — act done with intention/knowledge to cause death
Culpable homicide not amounting to murder — causing death without full mens rea of murder
offences.crimeSubHead: Culpable Homicide not amounting to Murder

offences.name: Voluntarily causing hurt — simple bodily injury (common in assault/affray FIRs)
Voluntarily causing grievous hurt — fracture, loss of limb/sight, injury endangering life etc.
Grievous hurt by dangerous weapons or means — knife, acid, fire, poison etc.; min 1 yr under BNS
Assault or criminal force on woman with intent to outrage her modesty
offences.crimeSubHead: Assault on Women with Intent to Outrage her Modesty

offences.name: Kidnapping — taking minor from lawful guardianship or conveying person beyond India
Kidnapping for ransom — abduction with threat of death/hurt to extort ransom
Rape — sexual assault as defined; min 10 yrs rigorous imprisonment
Theft — dishonest taking of movable property; petty theft (<Rs.5,000, first offence) can attract community service under BNS
Theft in a dwelling house or means of transportation — aggravated theft
Snatching — sudden/quick/forcible seizing of movable property from a person; NEW distinct offence in BNS (no IPC equivalent, earlier booked as 379/356 IPC); up to 3 yrs
Robbery — theft or extortion with violence or fear of instant hurt/death
Dacoity — robbery by five or more persons conjointly
House-breaking / lurking house-trespass in order to commit an offence (day burglary)
House-breaking by night (after sunset, before sunrise) to commit an offence — classic night burglary; up to 14 yrs if intended offence is theft
Criminal breach of trust — dishonest misappropriation of entrusted property
Cheating and dishonestly inducing delivery of property — the classic '420' fraud
Forgery — making a false document/electronic record to cause damage or fraud
Forgery of valuable security, will etc. — aggravated forgery, up to life imprisonment
Criminal intimidation — threat of injury to person, reputation or property; 351(3) if threat is of death/grievous hurt
Rioting — use of force/violence by an unlawful assembly
Rioting armed with deadly weapon
Dowry death — death of woman by burns/injury within 7 years of marriage with prior dowry cruelty; min 7 yrs
Cruelty by husband or his relatives — harassment/cruelty of married woman (cruelty defined in BNS 86)
offences.crimeSubHead: Cruelty by Husband or his Relatives

offences.name: Causing death by negligence — rash/negligent act causing death (fatal road accidents); up to 5 yrs under BNS (was 2 yrs under IPC)
offences.crimeSubHead: Causing Death by Negligence (incl. Road Accidents / Hit & Run)

offences.name: Rash driving or riding on a public way — endangering life or safety; usual accident-FIR section with MV Act
offences.crimeSubHead: Rash / Negligent Driving on Public Way

offences.name: Mischief — causing wrongful loss/damage to property (IPC 427 damage-value variant maps to BNS 324(4))
Criminal trespass — unlawful entry on property with intent to commit offence/intimidate/annoy
House-trespass — criminal trespass into a dwelling or building

acts.name: Narcotic Drugs and Psychotropic Substances Act

commonSections.desc: Prohibition of production/possession/sale/transport of narcotic drugs and psychotropic substances (cited with punishing sections)
Offences relating to cannabis (ganja) — punishment scales by small/intermediate/commercial quantity
Manufactured drugs and preparations (e.g. heroin, MDMA preparations) — quantity-scaled punishment
Psychotropic substances — quantity-scaled punishment
Consumption of narcotic drug or psychotropic substance
Financing illicit traffic and harbouring offenders
Abetment and criminal conspiracy
Licence required for acquisition/possession of firearms (cited with s.25)
Possession/manufacture/sale of arms or ammunition without licence
Punishment for using arms in contravention of s.5
Computer-related offences — hacking, data theft, dishonest damage to computer resource
Identity theft — fraudulent use of password/digital signature/unique ID (OTP frauds)
Cheating by personation using computer resource (most-cited section in cyber-fraud FIRs, with BNS 318(4))
Violation of privacy — capturing/publishing private images
Publishing/transmitting obscene material; sexually explicit material; child sexual abuse material

acts.name: Protection of Children from Sexual Offences Act

commonSections.desc: Punishment for penetrative sexual assault on child (min 10 yrs; cited with BNS 64/65)
Aggravated penetrative sexual assault (min 20 yrs to life/death)
Punishment for sexual assault (non-penetrative, 3-5 yrs)
Aggravated sexual assault (5-7 yrs)
Punishment for sexual harassment of a child
Abetment of POCSO offence
Dangerous driving (cited with BNS 281 / old IPC 279)
Driving under influence of alcohol/drugs (drunken driving)
Duty of driver in accident — failure to give aid/report (hit-and-run FIRs, r/w s.187)
Punishment for offences relating to accident (non-compliance with s.132/133/134)
Driving uninsured vehicle
Penalty for giving or taking dowry (min 5 yrs) — cited with BNS 85 / IPC 498A
Penalty for demanding dowry (6 months to 2 yrs)
Dowry to be for benefit of the wife — failure to transfer

acts.name: Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes (Prevention of Atrocities) Act

commonSections.desc: Intentional insult or intimidation with intent to humiliate SC/ST member in public view
Abuse by caste name in public view
IPC/BNS offence punishable with 10+ yrs committed against SC/ST person on caste ground — enhanced to life
Listed IPC/BNS offences against SC/ST members — enhanced punishment
Opening, etc., of certain forms of gaming — matka/satta betting, bookmaking (78(1)(a) etc. cited in gambling raids)
Keeping a common gaming-house
Gaming in a common gaming-house (persons found playing)
Gaming in public streets — petty street gambling (cards, dice)
Penalty for illegal import/export/transport/manufacture/sale/possession of intoxicants (bootlegging FIRs; up to 5 yrs RI)
Penalty for possession of intoxicant unlawfully imported/manufactured or duty-unpaid
Penalty for owner/occupier knowingly permitting premises/vehicle to be used for excise offences
Causing explosion likely to endanger life or property
Attempt to cause explosion, or making/keeping explosive with intent to endanger life/property
Making or possessing explosives under suspicious circumstances
Abetment
Public servant obtaining/accepting undue advantage (bribe) — typical Lokayukta trap FIR section
Taking undue advantage to influence public servant by corrupt means
Bribing a public servant (bribe-giver liability)
Criminal misconduct — misappropriation / assets disproportionate to known sources of income
Abetment of offences under the Act
Keeping or managing a brothel, or allowing premises to be used as one
Living on the earnings of prostitution
Procuring, inducing or taking person for prostitution
Detaining a person in premises where prostitution is carried on
Prostitution in or in the vicinity of public places
Seducing or soliciting for purpose of prostitution

crimeHeads.head: Offences Affecting the Human Body
subHeads: Culpable Homicide not amounting to Murder
Assault on Women with Intent to Outrage her Modesty
Causing Death by Negligence (incl. Road Accidents / Hit & Run / Medical Negligence)

crimeHeads.head: Crimes Against Women
subHeads: Assault on Women with Intent to Outrage her Modesty (incl. Sexual Harassment, Voyeurism, Stalking)
Insult to the Modesty of Women
Cruelty by Husband or his Relatives
Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act

crimeHeads.head: Crimes Against SC/ST
subHeads: SC/ST (Prevention of Atrocities) Act r/w BNS/IPC offences

crimeHeads.head: Cyber Crimes
subHeads: Cheating by Personation / Online Fraud (66D r/w BNS 318(4))

crimeHeads.head: Miscellaneous IPC/BNS Crimes
subHeads: Rash / Negligent Driving on Public Way

notes: Verification: cutover date and BNS enactment verified against India Code (indiacode.nic.in, Act 45 of 2023), PRS Legislative Research and Wikipedia. All IPC-to-BNS subsection mappings verified against the official government comparative table (BNS-IPC Corresponding Section Table PDF published on uppolice.gov.in, extracted and read directly) — including 302→103(1), 307→109, 304→105, 323→115(2), 325→117(2), 326→118(2), 354→74, 363→137(2), 364A→140(2), 376→64, 379→303(2), 380→305, snatching→304 (new), 392→309(4), 395→310(2), 454→331(3), 457→331(4), 406→316(2), 420→318(4), 465→336(2), 468→336(3), 467→338, 506→351(2)/(3), 147→191(2), 148→191(3), 304B→80, 498A→85, 304A→106(1), 279→281, 426→324(2), 427→324(4), 447→329(3), 448→329(4). Karnataka Police Act ss.78/79/80/87 titles verified from the full Act text (policehumanrightsresources.org PDF); post-2021 amendment these gambling offences (incl. online betting) are cognizable and punishments were enhanced (up to 3 yrs for 79/80). Karnataka Excise Act ss.32/34/38A verified via India Code and Indian Kanoon. FIR realism tips: Karnataka FIRs (CCTNS/Police IT) cite sections as e.g. 'u/s 303(2), 331(4) BNS' after 1 July 2024 and 'u/s 379, 457 IPC' before; accident FIRs typically read 'u/s 281, 106(1) BNS r/w 134(a)(b), 187 MV Act'; cyber-fraud FIRs read 'u/s 318(4) BNS r/w 66C, 66D IT Act'. Gravity caveat: NCRB does not publish a formal heinous definition (its Principal Offence Rule counts the offence with maximum punishment per FIR); the only statutory definition is JJ Act 2015 s.2(33) (min 7 yrs). The Heinous/Non-Heinous tags follow standard Karnataka police/SCRB crime-review practice (murder, attempt to murder, culpable homicide, rape, POCSO, kidnapping, dacoity, robbery, dowry death and grievous hurt by dangerous weapons treated as heinous; property, economic, matrimonial-cruelty and negligence offences as non-heinous) — house-breaking by night and IPC 467 forgery are borderline and sometimes shown as grave/serious. Crime-head taxonomy is an NCRB 'Crime in India'-style adaptation (heads overlap: e.g. rape appears under both Human Body and Crimes Against Women; NCRB counts each crime once by principal offence). BNS 106(2) (hit-and-run, up to 10 yrs) is notified but NOT in force per the official comparative table — do not cite it in generated FIRs. Unverified/lower-confidence items: exact MHA notification numbers omitted intentionally; Karnataka Excise punishment quanta reflect amended figures from secondary sources.
